title: Find Remainder

content.problemStatement: You are given a positive integer NNN. Your task is to find the last digit of NNN, i.e., the remainder when NNN is divided by 10.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the last digit of N (i.e., N mod 10).

sampleTestCases.explanation: The last digit of 70 is 0, which is the remainder when 70 is divided by 10.
The last digit of 55 is 5, which is the remainder when 55 is divided by 10.
The last digit of 123 is 3, which is the remainder when 123 is divided by 10.

solution.approach: The last digit of a number is exactly the remainder when the number is divided by 10. For each test case we read N and output N % 10. This operation is O(1) per test case and works for any size fitting in 64‑bit signed integer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides input and output buffers.

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # Example generator for hidden test cases (i from 1 to 20)
    if i == 1:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(1)
    elif i == 2:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(2)
    elif i == 3:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(9)
    elif i == 4:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(10)
    elif i == 5:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(1000000000)
    elif i == 6:
        t = 2
        print(t)
        print(1000000000)
        print(999999999)
    elif i == 7:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(999999999)
    elif i == 8:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(123456789)
    elif i == 9:
        t = 5
        print(t)
        for _ in range(t):
            print(7)
    elif i == 10:
        t = 4
        print(t)
        print(2)
        print(3)
        print(2)
        print(3)
    elif i == 11:
        t = 3
        print(t)
        print(1)
        print(2)
        print(3)
    elif i == 12:
        t = 3
        print(t)
        print(3)
        print(2)
        print(1)
    elif i == 13:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(999999937)
    elif i == 14:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(999999983)
    elif i == 15:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(500000000)
    elif i == 16:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(100000001)
    elif i == 17:
        t = 10
        print(t)
        for val in [1,10,20,30,40,50,60,70,80,90]:
            print(val)
    elif i == 18:
        t = 6
        print(t)
        print(1)
        print(1000000000)
        print(2)
        print(999999999)
        print(3)
        print(100)
    elif i == 19:
        t = 3
        print(t)
        print(123456789)
        print(987654321)
        print(555555555)
    elif i == 20:
        t = 15
        print(t)
        for val in range(1,16):
            print(val)
    else:
        pass
